title: UPST Q3 Earnings Preview: Should You Buy the Stock Now or Wait?
text: Upstart Holdings (UPST) reported Q3 2025 results on November 4, 2025. Management guided for revenues of $280 million and adjusted net income of approximately $44 million. Consensus Expectations: The Zacks Consensus Estimate projected quarterly revenues at $281.02 million (up 73.3% year-over-year) with earnings of 42 cents per share, compared to a loss of 6 cents in the prior year. Key Growth Drivers: The company's AI-based credit platform, particularly Model 22, showed strong performance. Lifted conversion rates from 19.1% in the first quarter to 23.9% in the second quarter, driving approval and origination volumes. The platform expanded into additional lending verticals including Auto and Home segments. Investment Recommendation: Analysts assigned a Hold rating (Zacks Rank #3). While the company demonstrated revenue rebound, renewed profitability and expansion into additional lending verticals, credit-sensitive exposure and market liquidity dependence created uncertainty.
